title: Token-only basis residual rerun under 512-step screening

queries: token-level residual alignment graph prompting llm tabular reasoning
graph-level prompt residual ablation basis alignment language model rerun

evidence.why_now: Across the completed basis-side bundles, token-only basis residual is still the strongest partial win: user-ltv improved and item-incoterms stayed neutral, while user-churn alone killed the bundle. After the screening protocol changed from 128 to 512, this is the highest-value candidate to rerun before inventing a more invasive code change.

hypothesis: Removing only graph-level basis residual injection while keeping token-level basis residual may still be the best narrow basis-side tradeoff. Under the more stable 512-step screening protocol, the previous user-churn regression may shrink enough to tell us whether this family deserves further work or should be retired completely.
notes: Prepared while waiting for the 512-step baseline refresh to finish.
baseline_registry.json has now been refreshed from EXP051/052/053.
Launched on 2026-05-05 under the 512-step strict screening protocol.
Run to completion under training-level early stop only; do not use bundle-level manual kill.
Completed on 2026-05-05 with global verdict: failed.
user-ltv improved, but user-churn and item-incoterms both regressed against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
